QCOM.title: Aaron Levie Says AI Agents Will Make Work More Accessible While Giving Experts A 'Huge Edge'

macro_events.event_summary: Saudi Aramco's Q1 profit surged 26% as its East-West pipeline (bypassing the Strait of Hormuz) hit full capacity amid the ongoing Iran war, signaling sustained high oil prices and constrained Gulf export logistics.
primary_drivers: Elevated crude prices from Iran war risk premium boosting upstream margins
Strait of Hormuz / Gulf shipping disruption rerouting flows and lifting tanker rates
Pipeline capacity bottleneck increasing value of non-Gulf supply sources

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated with significant upstream leverage; Aramco's 26% profit jump is a direct readthrough — ExxonMobil's Permian and Guyana barrels capture the same crude price uplift without Gulf transit risk.
Upstream-heavy major with Permian/Kazakhstan production; benefits from same Brent-linked pricing driving Aramco results, and Tengiz volumes route via non-Hormuz channels.
Pure-play Permian E&P with high oil cut and low breakeven; maximum torque to sustained $90+ Brent that the Aramco print confirms.
Domestic onshore E&P, zero Gulf transit exposure, leveraged to elevated oil prices implied by the war premium and pipeline-bottlenecked Saudi exports.
Frontline tanker fleet directly benefits from Hormuz disruption — Aramco pipeline 'at capacity' means residual barrels need longer-haul tanker routing, driving VLCC day rates.
International Seaways crude tanker exposure; same dynamic as FRO — rerouted Gulf cargoes and war-risk insurance premia on Hormuz transit lift spot rates.
Targa midstream operates US Gulf Coast NGL/crude export infrastructure; tightness in Saudi pipeline capacity increases premium for reliable non-Mideast export pathways.
Cheniere LNG exports become more strategically valuable as Gulf energy logistics strain; European/Asian buyers diversify away from Hormuz-routed cargoes.

losers.rationale: Delta has highest jet fuel exposure among US carriers and limited hedging; sustained crude strength confirmed by Aramco print directly compresses Q2 margins.
United similarly unhedged on fuel; Brent staying elevated due to Iran war premium is a direct cost hit to CASM-ex.
Southwest's traditional fuel hedge program has thinned; high oil price environment pressures unit costs against price-sensitive leisure demand.
Ethane/naphtha feedstock costs track crude; sustained high oil environment compresses petrochemical spreads on top of weak demand.
LyondellBasell olefins margins squeezed when crude-linked feedstocks stay elevated without commensurate polymer pricing power.
Fuel surcharges lag actual cost increases; sustained jet/diesel strength from Iran war premium pressures express margins.

headlines: Saudi Aramco Q1 profit jumps 26% as key pipeline reaches capacity amid Iran war
